title: Self-Correcting UI: Audio, Layout, Type & Color That Measure Themselves
description: Five patterns that react to and re-measure real data — audio bins, flex widths, word geometry, cursor angle, and contrast ratios.

drops.title: Audio-Reactive Kinetic Type
drops.description: A self-synthesized loop drives an AnalyserNode; each letter scales, lifts, and recolors from its own frequency bin, live.
drops.concept: No external audio file is needed — a chain of OscillatorNodes with gain envelopes sequences a bass-and-lead loop entirely in code. An AnalyserNode (fftSize 256) feeds getByteFrequencyData into a per-frame loop that maps each headline letter to a bin index and writes the normalized value into --level, which CSS calc() turns into scale, lift, hue and glow. A second mode reuses the same data to draw a canvas spectrum bar panel, and an optional microphone toggle swaps the source live.
drops.source: MDN — Visualizations with Web Audio API

interaction.mouse: Hover the mode tabs to switch between kinetic headline and spectrum panel views
interaction.click: Click Play to start the internal synth loop (AudioContext only starts on this gesture); switch waveform, tempo, or source in the debug panel
interaction.touch: All controls are tap-sized; works identically on mobile, including the optional mic toggle

drops.title: Self-Balancing Flex Gallery
drops.description: flex-wrap: balance (with a JS bin-packing fallback) reflows wrapped rows so the last line is never a lonely orphan.
drops.concept: Default flex-wrap: wrap greedily fills each row left to right, often leaving one small orphan item stranded on the final line. Where the new flex-wrap: balance CSS value is supported it's used directly via @supports; everywhere else a JS pass measures natural item widths, greedily assigns each item to whichever simulated row currently has the least filled width, and reorders the DOM via CSS order so wrapped rows read visually even. A width slider and a wrap/balance toggle per gallery make the redistribution obvious across a thumbnail wall, a tag cloud, and a pricing-card row.

interaction.mouse: Drag the container-width slider to force new wrap points; click Plain wrap / Balance under each gallery to compare
interaction.click: Toggle buttons switch each gallery's layout mode independently
interaction.touch: Slider and toggle buttons are touch-draggable/tappable; debug panel hides on small screens

drops.title: FLIP Word-Flip Headline
drops.description: Word-level FLIP animation: the pill's width snaps back and smoothly replays to each new word's natural size as it rotates in.
drops.concept: On every word swap the pill's current width is captured (First), the DOM text is swapped to the next word (Last), the width is instantly reset inline to the old value with no transition (Invert), and finally the inline override is cleared so the width transitions to its new natural size over a springy cubic-bezier (Play) — while the outgoing word rotates out on rotateX and the incoming word rotates in, so the colored pill visibly resizes in lockstep with the flip. Three headlines cycle independent word lists at different speeds and colors, with a global speed multiplier in the debug panel.

interaction.mouse: Purely ambient — watch the three headlines auto-cycle; drag the debug-panel slider to speed up or slow down all three at once
interaction.click: No click required to trigger cycling
interaction.touch: Fully ambient on touch too; debug panel hidden on small screens

drops.title: Direction-Aware Hover Cards
drops.description: Each card's reveal panel slides in and out through whichever edge the cursor actually crossed, computed live every time.
drops.concept: On mouseenter and mouseleave, cursor position is normalized to local (x, y) coordinates inside the card's bounding box, then two diagonal line equations (d1 = h·x − w·y and d2 = h·x + w·y − w·h) split the rectangle into four triangles; the sign combination of d1/d2 identifies top, right, bottom, or left. That direction sets which off-screen transform the reveal layer starts from and transitions to, and because the calculation re-runs on mouseleave using the exit point, the panel always retreats through the edge the cursor is currently near rather than a hardcoded default. Five cards demonstrate it independently, with a basic tap-to-toggle fallback for touch.
drops.source: Codrops — Direction-Aware Hover Effect with CSS3 and jQuery (2012 classic technique)

interaction.mouse: Hover any card from any edge — the reveal panel enters and exits through that same edge
interaction.click: No click needed on desktop; hover alone drives the effect
interaction.touch: Tap a card to toggle a default bottom-edge reveal since hover doesn't apply on touch

drops.title: Auto-Contrast Badge Composer
drops.description: One shared accent color feeds three live components; text color self-corrects for contrast automatically as the hue changes.
drops.concept: A single --accent custom property, set from a hue-range slider, drives a badge row, an alert banner, and a filled button, each using background: var(--accent) with color: contrast-color(var(--accent)) so the label always resolves to whichever of white or black wins WCAG-AA contrast against that background. A color-mix(in oklch, var(--accent), black 25%) border keeps a coherent darker edge. Where contrast-color() isn't supported, an @supports check swaps in a JS relative-luminance calculation as the fallback, and a Copy CSS button copies the current accent plus derived declarations to the clipboard.
drops.source: Smashing Magazine — Algorithmic Theming Engines: Building Self-Correcting Color Systems With contrast-color()

interaction.mouse: Drag the hue slider to retint all three components at once; watch labels stay readable automatically
interaction.click: Click Copy CSS to copy the current accent and derived declarations to the clipboard
interaction.touch: Slider is touch-draggable; buttons are tap-sized
